item.title: India’s Top Bank in $1 Billion Funding for Sun Pharma’s US Deal
item.description: State Bank of India plans to join a group of global lenders in funding Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd.’s $12 billion overseas acquisition, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Ukraine Estimates $11 Billion in Farm Losses From Russia Strikes
item.description: Ukraine’s agricultural sector suffered direct losses of more than $11 billion as Russian drone strikes intensified sharply at the end of last year, according to Deputy Economy Minister Taras Vysotskyi.

item.title: Iconiq-Backed IT Startup NinjaOne Hits $12.3 Billion Valuation
item.description: NinjaOne, an IT management platform, has more than doubled its valuation to $12.3 billion in a new financing deal, bucking the trend of investors shying away from software-as-a-service companies over AI fears. The startup is set to announce Tuesday that it’s raised $400 million in a secondary share sale. Wellington Management, Iconiq Capital, Alphabet Inc.’s CapitalG and Sequoia Capital participated in the deal, which saw some early investors and longtime employees sell equity. NinjaOne was prev

item.title: South African Economic Growth Beats Forecasts Despite War Hit
item.description: South Africa’s economy grew at a faster pace in the first quarter, shrugging off the initial headwinds from the Iran war even as manufacturing continued to lag.

item.title: Bank of Canada Likely to Hold After Weak First Quarter Data: Decision Guide
item.description: The Bank of Canada is widely expected to continue holding its policy interest rate for a fifth consecutive meeting, after recent data pointed to a weaker economy than the central bank had projected.

item.title: Intesa CEO Braces for Potential Bidding War Over Monte Paschi
item.description: Intesa Sanpaolo SpA Chief Executive Officer Carlo Messina signaled he’s prepared to address any counterbids for Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena SpA, after unveiling an offer that valued the world’s oldest bank at more than €30 billion ($35 billion).

item.title: Emerging-Market Stocks Rise Most in Two Months on AI Dip-Buying
item.description: Emerging-market stocks rebounded, led by South Korea, as investors snapped up artificial intelligence shares at lower prices after Monday’s selloff. Developing-nation currencies advanced after Iran and Israel paused hostilities.

item.title: Citi’s Chew Sees Aggressive Short-Selling Positions in US Stocks
item.description: Traders are aggressively building short-selling positions in US stocks, while bullish wagers on the tech sector remain stretched, putting the market at risk, according to Citigroup Inc. strategists.

item.title: Indonesia May Hike Rate Again to Shield Currency, Analysts Say
item.description: Indonesia may raise interest rate again to shield the rupiah and curb market volatility, according to analysts, after the central bank’s surprise tightening on Tuesday helped the currency rebound from record lows and boosted stocks.

item.title: SpaceX, Anthropic, OpenAI Can Rewrite History for Megacap IPOs
item.description: The history of megacap initial public offerings shows that the stocks usually slump in the first year of trading. But upcoming listings from SpaceX, Anthropic and OpenAI are big enough and systemically important enough to the market that those analogies may not apply.

item.title: Intesa CEO Says Monte Paschi Could Open Path to European Deals
item.description: Intesa Sanpaolo SpA Chief Executive Officer Carlo Messina said a successful takeover on Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena SpA would position his bank to engage in deals across Europe.

item.title: China Prepares $295 Billion Plan to Fund Nationwide AI Buildout
item.description: China is preparing to spend around 2 trillion yuan ($295 billion) over the next five years on building data centers across the country, fueling Beijing’s ambition to propel the domestic AI sector and surpass the US in a potentially game-changing technology.

item.title: Apple Expands Child Safety Tools as Social Media Bans Grow
item.description: Apple Inc. is expanding tools for parents to protect children online, a move that comes as governments around the world increasingly ban social media for young people.

item.title: Basque Region Raising €500 Million Bond for Industrial Strategy
item.description: The Basque government is raising €500 million ($577 million) from bonds to support the Spanish region’s industrial investment strategy.

item.title: Senegal Faces Fresh Debt Scrutiny Without IMF Deal, S&P Warns
item.description: Senegal’s public finances are under pressure and failure to win fresh support from the International Monetary Fund will harden concerns about its outlook, S&P Global Ratings warned.

item.title: Treasury Market Is Telling Kevin Warsh Rates Need to Be Higher
item.description: The $31 trillion Treasury market has an unequivocal message for Kevin Warsh’s Federal Reserve: Interest rates aren’t high enough.

item.title: Iceye Valuation Jumps to €10 Billion in Funding Round Led by General Atlantic
item.description: Iceye Oy quadrupled its valuation to more than €10 billion ($11.5 billion) in a General Atlantic-led funding round that will help the Finnish satellite-intelligence company expand to meet demand for sovereign technology systems.

item.title: Korea Readies On-Site FX Inspections to Curb Won Speculation
item.description: South Korea is set to crack down on speculative foreign-exchange trading, with regulators planning on-site inspections and investigations into suspected market-disruptive activities, the finance ministry said.

item.title: Broadband Firms’ Land Grab Backfired and Banks Are Selling Out
item.description: Banks with exposure to heavily-indebted broadband providers are selling their loans to distressed debt funds at a discount, a sign of their increasing weariness with the cash-strapped sector.

item.title: Vedanta Readies $5.2 Billion Refinancing After Rating Upgrades
item.description: Billionaire Anil Agarwal’s Vedanta Resources Ltd. is set to refinance $5.2 billion of US dollar bonds and loans, according to people familiar with the matter, as it seeks to lower borrowing costs by replacing expensive debt after securing credit-rating upgrades.

item.title: German Industry Output Sees First Gain Since Iran War Began
item.description: German industrial production grew for the first time since war broke out in Iran, fueling hope that Europe’s largest economy is weathering the jump in energy costs.

item.title: Saudi Contractor MGC Postpones $799 Million Riyadh IPO
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s Mutlaq Al-Ghowairi Contracting Co. has postponed plans for an initial public offering that was set to raise as much as $799 million and rank as the region’s first major listing of the year.

item.title: BlackRock to Offer Space ETF That Will Add New IPOs Within Days
item.description: BlackRock Inc. is launching a new space ETF for European investors that will add newly listed companies within 10 to 30 days.

item.title: Trump Says Peace Talks on Track After Israel-Iran Clashes End
item.description: US President Donald Trump renewed his claims of momentum toward ending the conflict with Iran, after brokering a halt to hostilities between Israel and the Islamic Republic and easing tensions that had threatened to derail broader peace efforts.

item.title: FTSE 100 Falls as Oil and Health Stocks Drag
Indonesia Bolsters Currency Defense With Surprise Rate Hike
item.description: Indonesia’s central bank announced a surprise interest rate hike, seeking to halt a currency freefall that risks stoking inflation.

item.title: Revego and H1 Mull Merger for $807 Million Africa Renewables Fund
item.description: Revego Fund Managers is exploring a merger with H1 Holdings that would create one of South Africa’s largest renewable energy-focused funds.

item.title: CIMB Says ‘Good Time’ to Invest in Battered Indonesia, Seeks M&A
item.description: CIMB Group Holdings Bhd., Malaysia’s second-biggest bank, says it’s seeking merger and acquisition opportunities in neighboring Indonesia despite a slump in investor confidence in Southeast Asia’s biggest economy.

item.title: Logistics Firm GLP Aims for $2 Billion of Asset Sales This Year
item.description: GLP Pte. told some bondholders during meetings in recent days that it aims to sell around $2 billion of assets this year, according to people familiar with the matter, as the logistics operator prepares for an initial public offering.

item.title: Poland Eyes New Growth Engines to Compete in Big Leagues, State Assets Minister Says
item.description: Poland wants its local companies to reap the rewards of a projected investment boom as the country seeks new growth drivers to propel its $1 trillion economy, State Assets Minister Wojciech Balczun said.
